text: An API allows different software applications to communicate with each other. When you work with an API, you typically send HTTP requests, such as GET to retrieve data or POST to send new information. The server often responds with JSON, a lightweight data format that is easy to read and process. Many APIs require authentication, usually through an API key, to ensure only authorized users can access the service. It is important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, good error handling is essential, as you should check for HTTP status codes like 404 or 500 to respond appropriately.
